findings.title: Claim citations can select a losing conflicting fact
findings.original_evidence: A mechanical MIT winner plus lower-precedence Proprietary documentation loser previously allowed a Proprietary claim citing the loser to validate.
findings.resolution_evidence: validate_claim_citations now requires each cited fact ID to equal selected_fact_ids for its field. Independent focused execution passed the lower-precedence and same-rank loser negative controls in tests/unit/test_facts_resolution.py.

findings.title: Metadata proposal capability trusts caller-supplied factuality gates and citations
findings.original_evidence: The capability previously accepted forgeable surface_eligibility and fact_citations arguments.
findings.resolution_evidence: The manifest and execute signature now accept only org_repo. The capability re-derives facts through facts.provider.collect_product_facts and computes eligibility/citations internally. Direct dispatch with forged arguments returns rejected_invalid_arguments. facts/provider.py imports no capability or dispatcher module, while get_product_facts and propose_metadata_changes both depend downward on the provider, so no capability-to-capability dispatch dependency or cycle was introduced.

findings.title: Metadata proposal module contract still documents the removed caller-supplied-facts architecture
findings.evidence: The capability module docstring accurately describes the shared provider architecture. The guarded module-map retrofit now makes both docs/architecture.md rows agree that metadata_presentation dispatches only propose_metadata_changes(org_repo), which independently derives facts through facts/provider.py. Current imports and dispatch wiring match those descriptions, and plan-structure validation is clean.

residual_blockers: The parent Wave 3 remains blocked by Wave 2 hosted GitHub App/LLM/recovery proof, full ProductFactsV2 ingestion for explicit missing fields, real isolated example execution, and external product-owner conflict resolution.
